name: sonicwall-nsm
label: SonicWall NSM
description: SonicWall Network Security Manager (NSM) is a cloud-based (or on-prem) platform designed to centrally manage, monitor, and report on SonicWall firewalls and security policies across distributed environments.

fields.title: Server URL
fields.name: server_url
fields.description: Specify the server URL of the SonicWall NSM server to connect and perform automated operations.
fields.tooltip: Specify the server URL of the SonicWall NSM server to connect and perform automated operations.

fields.title: API Key
fields.name: api_key
fields.tooltip: Specify the API Key to connect to the endpoint and perform automated operations.
fields.description: Specify the API Key to connect to the endpoint and perform automated operations.

fields.title: Verify SSL
fields.name: verify_ssl
fields.description: Specifies whether the SSL certificate for the server is to be verified. By default, this option is selected, i.e., set to true
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the SSL certificate for the server is to be verified. By default, this option is selected, i.e., set to true

operations.title: Get Access Rules
operations.description: Retrieve the list of access rules between the from and to zones from SonicWall NSM.

parameters.title: Filter Type
parameters.name: filterType
parameters.description: Select the filter type to retrieve access rules. You can choose from the following options: From Zone to To Zone, Source Address to Destination Address, or Source Port to Destination Port
parameters.tooltip: Select the filter type to retrieve access rules. You can choose from the following options: From Zone to To Zone, Source Address to Destination Address, or Source Port to Destination Port

Source Zone to Destination Zone.title: Source Zone
Source Zone to Destination Zone.name: from
Source Zone to Destination Zone.description: Specify the source zone to filter access rules. Note: If you specify "Any" all values need to be considered.
Source Zone to Destination Zone.tooltip: Specify the source zone to filter access rules. Note: If you specify "Any" all values need to be considered.
Source Zone to Destination Zone.placeholder: For e.g LAN, WAN, DMZ, VPN, WLAN, MULTICAST, or Any

Source Zone to Destination Zone.title: Destination Zone
Source Zone to Destination Zone.name: to
Source Zone to Destination Zone.description: Specify the destination zone to filter access rules. Note: If you specify "Any" all values need to be considered.
Source Zone to Destination Zone.tooltip: Specify the destination zone to filter access rules.  Note: If you specify "Any" all values need to be considered.
Source Zone to Destination Zone.placeholder: For e.g LAN, WAN, DMZ, VPN, WLAN, MULTICAST, or Any

Source Address to Destination Address.title: From Address
Source Address to Destination Address.name: from
Source Address to Destination Address.description: Specify the source address to filter access rules. If you are using address object names, they must exist in the firewall configuration. Note: If you specify "Any", all values will be considered.
Source Address to Destination Address.tooltip: Specify the source address to filter access rules. If you are using address object names, they must exist in the firewall configuration. Note: If you specify "Any", all values will be considered.

Source Address to Destination Address.title: Destination Address
Source Address to Destination Address.name: to
Source Address to Destination Address.description: Specify the destination address to filter access rules. If you are using address object names, they must exist in the firewall configuration. Note: If you specify "Any", all values will be considered.
Source Address to Destination Address.tooltip: Specify the destination address to filter access rules. If you are using address object names, they must exist in the firewall configuration. Note: If you specify "Any", all values will be considered.

Source Port to Destination Port.title: Source Port
Source Port to Destination Port.name: from
Source Port to Destination Port.description: Specify the source port to filter access rules. Note: If you specify "Any" all values need to be considered.
Source Port to Destination Port.tooltip: Specify the source port to filter access rules. Note: If you specify "Any" all values need to be considered.

Source Port to Destination Port.title: Destination Port
Source Port to Destination Port.name: to
Source Port to Destination Port.description: Specify the destination port to filter access rules. Note: If you specify "Any" all values need to be considered.
Source Port to Destination Port.tooltip: Specify the destination port to filter access rules. Note: If you specify "Any" all values need to be considered.

parameters.title: Device Serial Number
parameters.name: serialnum
parameters.description: Specify the device serial number to filter access rules.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the device serial number to filter access rules.

parameters.title: Rule Type
parameters.name: ruleType
parameters.description: Select the rule type to retrieve access rules. You can choose from the following options: Access Rules or Security Policies. By default, it set as "Access Rules"
parameters.tooltip: Select the rule type to retrieve access rules. You can choose from the following options: Access Rules or Security Policies. By default, it set as "Access Rules"

parameters.title: Limit
parameters.description: If a specific number of child relationships is required, check the limit option. To retrieve all values, leave the option unchecked.
parameters.name: limit
parameters.tooltip: If a specific number of child relationships is required, check the limit option. To retrieve all values, leave the option unchecked.

parameters.title: Limit Total Count
parameters.description: Specifies the limitCount value is used to determine how many child relationships are required. This parameter helps control the volume of data by limiting the total count of items in the response.
parameters.name: limitCount
parameters.tooltip: Specifies the limitCount value is used to determine how many child relationships are required. This parameter helps control the volume of data by limiting the total count of items in the response.

parameters.title: Children Object Types
parameters.name: childrenObjTypes
parameters.description: Specify the children object type to filter access rules.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the children object type to filter access rules.

operations.title: Create Address Object
operations.description: Update an existing address object on a SonicWall NSM server based on the parameters you have specified.

parameters.title: Address Object Name
parameters.name: name
parameters.tooltip: Specify the name for the address object you want to update.
parameters.description: Specify the name for the address object you want to update.

parameters.title: Zone
parameters.name: zone
parameters.tooltip: Specify the security zone that the address object belongs to on the SonicWall NSM server.
parameters.description: Specify the security zone that the address object belongs to on the SonicWall NSM server.
parameters.placeholder: For e.g LAN, WAN, VPN, etc.

parameters.title: Object Type
parameters.name: object_type
parameters.description: Select the object type to update the address object in SonicWall NSM server. You can choose from the following options: Host (Single IP Address), Network (IP/Subnet), Range (Start IP - End IP), FQDN (Domain Name), or MAC Address.
parameters.tooltip: Select the object type to update the address object in SonicWall NSM server. You can choose from the following options: Host (Single IP Address), Network (IP/Subnet), Range (Start IP - End IP), FQDN (Domain Name), or MAC Address.

Host (Single IP Address).title: IP Address
Host (Single IP Address).name: ip
Host (Single IP Address).tooltip: Specify the IP address of the host you want to define.
Host (Single IP Address).description: Specify the specific IP address of the host to be added as an address object.

Network (IP/Subnet).title: IP Address
Network (IP/Subnet).name: ip
Network (IP/Subnet).tooltip: Specify the network IP address (e.g., the base of a subnet).
Network (IP/Subnet).description: Specify the base IP address of the network to be used for this address object.

Network (IP/Subnet).title: Netmask
Network (IP/Subnet).name: netmask
Network (IP/Subnet).tooltip: Specify the subnet mask to define the network range.
Network (IP/Subnet).description: Specify the subnet mask associated with the network address.

Range (Start IP - End IP).title: Starting Range of IP Address
Range (Start IP - End IP).name: start_ip
Range (Start IP - End IP).tooltip: Specify the starting IP address of the range.
Range (Start IP - End IP).description: Specify the starting IP address of the range.

Range (Start IP - End IP).title: Ending Range of IP Address
Range (Start IP - End IP).name: end_ip
Range (Start IP - End IP).tooltip: Specify the ending IP address of the range.
Range (Start IP - End IP).description: Specify the ending IP address of the range.

FQDN (Domain Name).title: Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN)
FQDN (Domain Name).name: fqdn
FQDN (Domain Name).tooltip: Specify the fully qualified domain name (FQDN) to resolve dynamically.
FQDN (Domain Name).description: Specify the DNS-resolvable domain name used to create this address object.

MAC Address.title: MAC Address
MAC Address.name: mac
MAC Address.tooltip: Specify the MAC address of the device to define this object.
MAC Address.description: Specify the physical (MAC) address of the device to associate with this object.

parameters.title: Description
parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Specify the description for the address object you want to create.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the description for the address object you want to create.

parameters.title: Device Serial Number
parameters.name: serialnum
parameters.description: Specify the device serial number to create the address object.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the device serial number to create the address object.

operations.title: Get Address Object
operations.description: Retrieve the list of connected items to address objects within the SonicWall NSM server.

parameters.title: Address Object Name/UUID
parameters.name: name
parameters.tooltip: Specify the name or UUID for the address object based on which you want to retrieve connected items.
parameters.description: Specify the name or UUID for the address object based on which you want to retrieve connected items.

parameters.title: Is UUID
parameters.name: isuuid
parameters.description: Uncheck this option if you are passing a address object name instead of a address object UUID.
parameters.tooltip: Set to false if providing a address object name instead of a address object UUID.

parameters.title: Device Serial Number
parameters.name: serialnum
parameters.description: Specify the device serial number to retrieve the address object.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the device serial number to retrieve the address object.

operations.title: Update Address Object
operations.description: Add a address object on a SonicWall NSM server based on the parameters you have specified.

parameters.title: Address Object UUID
parameters.name: uuid
parameters.description: Specify the UUID for the address object you want to update.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the UUID for the address object you want to update.

parameters.title: Address Object Name
parameters.name: name
parameters.tooltip: Specify the name for the address object you want to update.
parameters.description: Specify the name for the address object you want to update.

parameters.title: Zone
parameters.name: zone
parameters.tooltip: Specify the security zone that the address object belongs to on the SonicWall NSM server.
parameters.description: Specify the security zone that the address object belongs to on the SonicWall NSM server.
parameters.placeholder: For e.g LAN, WAN, VPN, etc.

parameters.title: Object Type
parameters.name: object_type
parameters.description: Select the object type to create the address object in SonicWall NSM server. You can choose from the following options: Host (Single IP Address), Network (IP/Subnet), Range (Start IP - End IP), FQDN (Domain Name), or MAC Address.
parameters.tooltip: Select the object type to create the address object in SonicWall NSM server. You can choose from the following options: Host (Single IP Address), Network (IP/Subnet), Range (Start IP - End IP), FQDN (Domain Name), or MAC Address.

Host (Single IP Address).title: IP Address
Host (Single IP Address).name: ip
Host (Single IP Address).tooltip: Specify the updated IP address assigned to this host address object.
Host (Single IP Address).description: Specify the IP address representing the host you want to update in the address object.

Network (IP/Subnet).title: IP Address
Network (IP/Subnet).name: ip
Network (IP/Subnet).tooltip: Specify the updated network base IP address for this object.
Network (IP/Subnet).description: Specify the base IP address of the network to update the network address object.

Network (IP/Subnet).title: Netmask
Network (IP/Subnet).name: netmask
Network (IP/Subnet).tooltip: Specify the updated subnet mask to define the network range.
Network (IP/Subnet).description: Specify the subnet mask to update the range of the network in the address object.

Range (Start IP - End IP).title: Starting Range of IP Address
Range (Start IP - End IP).name: start_ip
Range (Start IP - End IP).tooltip: Specify the new starting IP address of the defined range.
Range (Start IP - End IP).description: Specify the starting IP address you want to update for the range address object.

Range (Start IP - End IP).title: Ending Range of IP Address
Range (Start IP - End IP).name: end_ip
Range (Start IP - End IP).tooltip: Specify the new ending IP address of the defined range.
Range (Start IP - End IP).description: Specify the ending IP address you want to update for the range address object.

FQDN (Domain Name).title: Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN)
FQDN (Domain Name).name: fqdn
FQDN (Domain Name).tooltip: Specify the updated domain name to be resolved dynamically via DNS.
FQDN (Domain Name).description: Specify the fully qualified domain name (FQDN) you want to update in the address object.

MAC Address.title: MAC Address
MAC Address.name: mac
MAC Address.tooltip: Specify the updated MAC address to associate with this object.
MAC Address.description: Specify the physical MAC address you want to update in the address object.

parameters.title: New Address Object Name
parameters.name: new_name
parameters.tooltip: Specify the new name for the address object you want to update in SonicWall NSM server.
parameters.description: Specify the new name for the address object you want to update in SonicWall NSM server.

parameters.title: Device Serial Number
parameters.name: serialnum
parameters.description: Specify the device serial number to update the address object.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the device serial number to update the address object.

operations.title: Execute an API Request
operations.description: Sends an API request to an API endpoint based on specified HTTP method, endpoint, and other input parameters that you have specified, enabling flexible API interactions tailored to user needs.

parameters.name: method
parameters.title: HTTP Method
parameters.description:
Select an HTTP action for the request. You can select from the following options:  

DELETE 

GET 

PATCH 

POST 

PUT

parameters.title: Endpoint
parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.tooltip: Specify the target API URL path for example: tenants
parameters.description: Specify the target API URL path for the request. For example, if the website is https://example.com and URL path is https://example.com/images/pic.jpg, the endpoint would be images/pic.jpg.

parameters.title: Query Parameters
parameters.name: query_params
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify any optional parameters to add to the URL and refine the request.

parameters.name: payload
parameters.title: Request Payload
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify data, as JSON, to be sent as the request payload (typically for POST or PUT requests).
